issues.description: logsToCsv serializes the entire payload object into the CSV Details cell. Existing ADMIN_ACTION payloads include internal identifiers such as targetMemberId and messageId, so the export can leak fields the WI explicitly calls out for CSV information hygiene.
issues.recommendation: Sanitize log payloads before CSV serialization, either by allowlisting display-safe keys per action/event type or by recursively omitting internal identifier keys such as memberId, targetMemberId, messageId, spaceId, userId, and guestSessionId.

issues.description: Member name fallback uses nullish coalescing, while the visible table and member filter use truthy fallback. If user.name or guestSession.nickname is an empty string, the CSV exports an empty Name instead of the same fallback value the dashboard displays.
issues.recommendation: Use the same fallback expression as MemberTable/filterMembers, for example m.user?.name || m.guestSession?.nickname || m.displayName || "Unknown" or a documented CSV-specific equivalent.

issues.description: downloadCsv does not clean up in a finally block. If anchor click or DOM removal throws, the temporary anchor can remain attached and the object URL may not be revoked.
issues.recommendation: Wrap the click/removal/revoke sequence in try/finally, revoking the URL whenever it was created and removing the anchor only if it was appended.
issues.deferRationale: Browser click failures are uncommon; this is robustness hardening rather than a primary functional break.

issues.description: Targeted Vitest verification could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite/esbuild failed at startup with spawn EPERM. Static validation did run: npm run lint -- --quiet and npx tsc --noEmit both passed.
issues.recommendation: Rerun the targeted Vitest command in an environment that permits esbuild child-process startup before merging.
issues.deferRationale: The failure is an execution-environment restriction observed during review, not direct evidence of a code defect.
